title: Bridge Essay: Revealing abrupt transitions from goal-directed to habitual behavior.
concept: Habit as automated behavior — loss of conscious control, automaticity, action monitoring.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.
**Phase context:** Habit as automated behavior — loss of conscious control, automaticity, action monitoring.

**Revealing abrupt transitions from goal-directed to habitual behavior.** — bioRxiv (2023). DOI: 10.1101/2023.07.05.547783. PMID: 37461576. A fundamental tenet of animal behavior is that decision-making involves multiple 'controllers.' Initially, behavior is goal-directed, driven by desired outcomes, shifting later to habitual control, where cues trigger actions independent of motivational state.
